picks.title: La Pegatina
picks.blurb: Catalan seven-piece returns after sabbatical; high-energy rumba catalana, ska and punk spectacle.

picks.title: Bar Dancing Multipla: Final Weekend
picks.blurb: Garage venue's last weekend: fogged doors, orange LED lighting, eclectic DJs (techno to house). Closure party.
picks.why: Final weekend of beloved garage nightclub.

picks.title: deLux: Lozado at Escape
picks.blurb: Escape's deLux room dedicated to Lozado. Intimate, luxurious club within the main venue with separate sound.

picks.title: Lemon
picks.blurb: Live music both days at casual Amsterdam hotel venue.

picks.title: Escape: House & Dub | deLux: Lozado
picks.blurb: High-energy Amsterdam clubbing icon with state-of-the-art sound. Mainroom house, deLux room house & dub.

picks.title: NextSteps: Ballet & Hip Hop Fusion
picks.blurb: Contemporary dance fusing ballet and hip-hop styles.

picks.title: Rumba Latina: Colombia vs Portugal LIVE FUTBOL
picks.blurb: World Cup-themed Latin night with live football screenings and DJ sets.

picks.title: Sunday Clay Club
picks.blurb: Relaxed hand-building and pottery wheel intro for beginners. Three-hour guided session in supportive, informal setting.
picks.why: Hands-on clay experience for all levels

picks.title: WOPL! Zomerfeest
picks.blurb: Family afternoon of summer fun with music, games, and good vibes for all ages.

picks.title: Canvas Rooftop Bar
picks.blurb: Enclosed 7th-floor rooftop with panoramic Amstel & city-centre views from dusk onward.

picks.title: Founders Running Club
picks.blurb: 3.3 km social run with Amsterdam's Founders Running Club. Meet at Cafe Conrad for a casual weekend start.
picks.why: Free weekend run club in the city

picks.title: IJ-Hallen Outdoor Market
picks.blurb: Europe's largest flea market: 750+ outdoor vendor stalls, vintage finds, food trucks. Weekend institution.

picks.title: Thuishaven w/ Joss Dean / S.A.M. (live) / Rooléh & more
picks.blurb: Waterfront day party featuring live acts and DJs in leafy Noord venue.

picks.title: Recycle Rally & Family Workshops
picks.blurb: Free recycling workshops & activities for kids and adults, ending with Recycle Rally finale.
picks.why: Free family event, Sunday afternoon activity

picks.title: Butterfly Kingdom at Black Cat Theatre
picks.blurb: Children's theatre production about butterflies—interactive, magical journey through nature.

picks.title: Family Guided Tour & Sketching in the Sculpture Hall
picks.blurb: Every weekend: family tours exploring the collection together, plus Sunday sketching sessions in the Sculpture Hall for all ages.
picks.why: Perfect for families; no booking needed beyond museum entry

picks.title: Recycle Summer Festival at Jeugdland
picks.blurb: Free family festival: recycling workshops, creative activities, sustainability focus, and Recycle Rally finale for kids and adults.
picks.why: All activities free; fun family focus

picks.title: Eye(s) Open: New Perspectives on Colonial Film Heritage
picks.when: Sat 27 Jun – Sun 28 Jun (ongoing until 6 Sep)
picks.blurb: Eleven contemporary artists respond to Eye's colonial-era Indonesian and Surinamese films with new commissions and installations.
picks.why: Bold reframing of colonial archives; artist-led dialogue

picks.title: Collection: Mid-Century Modernism & Contemporary Works
picks.blurb: Kho Liang Ie's design retrospective (until Oct 18) sits alongside Experimental Jetset's graphic circuits and Farida Sedoc's social-capital inquiry.
picks.why: Stellar permanent collection; multiple strong contemporary s

picks.title: ARTIS Aquarium & Summer Activities
picks.blurb: New ARTIS Aquarium alongside splashing summer activities centred on water and marine life.

picks.title: Amsterdam Roots Festival
picks.blurb: 44-year-old global arts festival: world music, circus, dance, theater, food vendors, workshops. LaLaLand curates Indian artists; MeentCoop spotlights community
picks.source: Amsterdam Roots Festival official · I amsterdam / Amsterdam Tips · I amsterdam, Amsterdam Roots official · Your Little Black Book · I amsterdam Weekend Guide · Amsterdam Roots Festival / I amsterdam

picks.title: Wallen Festival
picks.venue: Red Light District & Old City Centre
picks.blurb: Multi-location festival with 80+ activities: live music, local food, performances, workshops, and guided tours through historic alleyways.
picks.why: Nearly 100 cultural venues open to locals and visitors
picks.source: Your Little Black Book Amsterdam · Gemeente Amsterdam / Wallen Festival · Your Little Black Book / I amsterdam · Gemeente Amsterdam / Waag

picks.title: IJ-Hallen Flea Market
picks.blurb: Europe's largest flea market with 750+ outdoor stalls: vintage, furniture, clothes, collectibles. Treasure hunt for unique finds.
picks.why: Massive scale, diverse goods, weekend hours
picks.source: Amsterdam Tips · AmsterdamTips · AmsterdamTips.com · Amsterdam Tips / IJ-Hallen

picks.title: The Maker Market
picks.blurb: Creative weekend market in covered passage: handmade jewelry, art, design, vintage goods, plus live music and food.

picks.title: Holland Festival
picks.blurb: Amsterdam's premier international theatre and music festival closes this weekend with cutting-edge performances across multiple venues.
picks.source: I amsterdam · Holland Festival / I amsterdam

picks.title: Food for Thought Festival
picks.blurb: Three-day multicultural festival celebrating Keti Koti with Caribbean soul food, live reggae (Half Pint, Eek-A-Mouse), DJs, spoken word, and art.
picks.why: Caribbean food, reggae legends, commemorative music
picks.source: Amsterdam Spotted, Food for Thought offi · Amsterdam Spotted / Reggae Agenda

picks.title: The Movies — arthouse cinema
picks.blurb: The Netherlands’ oldest cinema still running — Art Deco rooms, a dinner-and-a-film deal, arthouse bills.
picks.why: Indoor · dinner + a film · perfect when it’s wet

picks.title: Huis Marseille
picks.blurb: The city’s first photography museum, across two canal houses with a hidden garden — beautifully curated, never crowded.

picks.title: Danh Vo – πνεῦμα (Ἔλισσα)
picks.blurb: Vietnamese-American artist's meditation on breath and memory closes Aug 2. Sunday introduction talk explores this poetic, haunting installation.
picks.why: Final weeks; artist-led insight into ambitious work

picks.title: East Beach — rooftop city beach
picks.blurb: A new 800m² rooftop city beach above Oosterpark — sand, beach chairs, drinks, open to all.

picks.title: Carel Visser — sculpture in the gardens
picks.blurb: Free outdoor sculpture show — the first artist solo in the Rijksmuseum gardens, opening this weekend.

picks.title: De Pimpelmees
picks.blurb: Cosy neighbourhood wine bar — European wines from €6, Italian snacks, free chips. Opened early 2026.

picks.title: In C – Dutch National Ballet
picks.blurb: Classical ballet performances from Dutch National Ballet dancers & Junior Company.

picks.title: Spinoza — De Mokum Musical
picks.blurb: Open-air musical about philosopher Spinoza set in old Amsterdam, in the forest theatre.
picks.why: Cross-source (LBB + I amsterdam) · open-air · dry-night plan

picks.title: IJ-Hallen
picks.blurb: 750 outdoor stands selling secondhand clothes, vintage goods, curiosities. Amsterdam's largest weekend flea market.

picks.title: Keti Koti: Naar Ndakaaru zwemmen
picks.blurb: Celebrate and commemorate Keti Koti with stories, conversations, food, and music for families.

picks.title: Queer Power
picks.when: Sat 27 Jun – Sun 28 Jun (ongoing until 2 Sep)
picks.blurb: Celebration of queer filmmakers worldwide during World Pride. Diverse perspectives, freedom and inclusivity at the centre.
picks.why: World Pride Amsterdam backdrop; fresh curatorial angle

picks.title: CIMA Rooftop Bar
picks.blurb: High rooftop with cooling specials & refreshing cocktails for sunset & evening drinks.
picks.why: Open Saturday only, cooler evening vibes

picks.title: Houthavens Eats
picks.blurb: Food-focused event at Houthavens' waterfront terrace. Curated culinary experience with local producers and street-food vendors.

picks.title: As
picks.blurb: Wood-fired, ingredient-led cooking and natural wine in a striking round garden pavilion.

picks.title: BAK
picks.blurb: A light-filled warehouse with an open kitchen and an art-covered wall; a set seasonal menu of produce-driven dishes.

picks.title: Foam
picks.blurb: A nimble photography museum in a canal house — big-name retrospectives and emerging talent, always worth the drop-in.

picks.title: Choux
picks.blurb: Inventive, veg-forward small plates and natural wine in a raw red-brick space near the IJ.

picks.title: Moise
picks.blurb: An understated De Pijp boutique with a razor-sharp eye — Acne, Lemaire, Marant and the like, hand-picked and beautifully shown.

picks.title: Patta
picks.blurb: The homegrown streetwear label and culture hub — drops, sneakers and the community that put Amsterdam on the map.

picks.title: Pllek — waterfront hang
picks.blurb: A cargo-container café-bar on the NDSM wharf — big windows onto the IJ, sofas, and a wood stove when it turns.
picks.why: Indoor seating · IJ views · cozy when grey

picks.title: RIJKS®
picks.blurb: Joris Bijdendijk’s Michelin-starred dining room beside the Rijksmuseum — the “cuisine of the Low Countries”, done with flair.

picks.title: X Bank
picks.blurb: A concept store in a former bank showing 180+ Dutch fashion, design and art labels — couture to hand-thrown ceramics.
picks.why: Best of Dutch design · one room

picks.title: Rijsel
picks.blurb: Lille-inspired French cooking in a converted school — that rotisserie chicken is the order.

picks.title: Bar Fisk
picks.blurb: Sun-soaked Mediterranean seafood and sharing plates in De Pijp.

picks.title: Haarlem — the easy escape
picks.blurb: A calmer historic city 15 min away — Grote Markt, the St-Bavo organ, a Saturday market and café-lined square.

picks.title: Mobilia
picks.blurb: Three floors of high design on the Utrechtsestraat — icons by the masters alongside more attainable covetables.

picks.title: Vlieger
picks.blurb: A two-floor temple to paper since 1869 — hundreds of colours, handmade sheets and card stacked to the ceiling.

picks.title: Café Chris — oldest brown café
picks.blurb: Open since 1624: a creaky, golden-lit Jordaan brown café for a slow afternoon beer while it pours outside.

picks.title: De Ceuvel
picks.blurb: A café on a reclaimed shipyard — a circular-economy experiment with a ramshackle, plant-filled terrace.

picks.title: Efteling
picks.blurb: Europe’s oldest fairytale theme park — enchanted forest, dark rides and proper coasters in a storybook world.

picks.title: Mata Hari
picks.blurb: A warm canal-side bar-restaurant on the Wallen — candlelit, characterful and a local antidote to the crowds.

picks.title: Kriterion
picks.blurb: A legendary student-run cinema and café since 1945 — arthouse programming with a buzzy terrace.

picks.title: Madurodam
picks.blurb: The whole of the Netherlands at 1:25 — miniature canals, Schiphol and windmills kids can tower over.

picks.title: Sauna Deco — Art Deco baths
picks.blurb: A 1920s Art Deco bathhouse (salvaged from a Paris department store) with saunas, steam and a quiet tea room.
picks.why: Warm · restorative · the ultimate cold-and-wet antidote

picks.title: Friday Next
picks.blurb: A concept store and café in one — young Dutch design to browse, with brunch and coffee while you do it.

picks.title: WonderWood
picks.blurb: A gallery-shop devoted to plywood and mid-century modern — bentwood chairs and design classics since 1999.

picks.title: Eye Classics
picks.blurb: Film history on the big screen: classics screened in 35mm whenever possible. A deep dive into cinema's greatest works.
picks.why: Celluloid experience; essential for film lovers

picks.title: Cowboys to Catwalk
picks.blurb: A treasure-trove of vintage designer — Margiela, Marc Jacobs and one-off finds rummaged from decades of fashion.

picks.title: Zaanse Schans windmills
picks.blurb: Eight working windmills, cheese and crafts on the Zaan — free grounds, ~17 min train from Centraal.
picks.why: Short train · outdoor · classic kid day
